s: Make it correct first, then fast. Profile before optimizing. 90% of issues from 10% of code.
Never empty catch. Every caught error must log context (message, stack, inputs). Silent failures are hardest to debug.
Never hardcode config (connection strings, API keys, URLs, magic numbers). Use env, config files, or secret managers.
DRY: extract shared logic into one module. Duplication multiplies maintenance cost linearly.
Every critical path (auth, payment, data mutation) must have automated tests. Proof code works.
Use strict equality === and explicit conversion. Implicit coercion creates subtle bugs.
Validate ALL external input at boundary (query, body, headers, files, env vars). Never trust client.
Keep domain logic framework-agnostic. Depend on abstractions (interfaces), not concrete framework types.
Batch DB queries. Eager loading (.Include), batch endpoints, or DataLoader.
Keep PRs under 400 lines. Small PRs reviewed thoroughly, merged faster, fewer conflicts.
Structured JSON logging with correlation IDs. Levels: Debug/Info/Warning/Error. Never log secrets or PII.
Dependency graph must be DAG. Break cycles with interfaces, events, or mediation.
Avoid mutable global/static state. Makes tests order-dependent. Use DI with scoped lifetimes.
Always parameterized queries or ORM. Never concatenate user input into SQL.
Network/DB/external API can fail transiently. Exponential backoff with jitter. Circuit breaker for non-idempotent.
Functions should do ONE thing. Over 50 lines = warning. Extract well-named helpers.
When non-critical dep fails, degrade gracefully. Return cached/default data, disable feature, queue retry.
Version API from day one (/v1/, Accept-Version header, content negotiation). Breaking changes without versioning break clients.
